summary: Moved the startup helper assembly routines __init_hardware and __flush_cache out of src/dolphin/os/init/__start.c and into src/dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.c, matching placement indicated by other games' debug maps. The change also moved declarations for __OSCacheInit and __OSPSInit from the private __start.h header into their relevant OSCache.h and OSInit.h headers so the relocated translation unit can include proper public/internal subsystem declarations.
what_changed: Added PPCArch.h, OSCache.h, and OSInit.h includes to __ppc_eabi_init.c and defined MSR_FP there so the moved assembly routines have the dependencies they previously had through __start.c or __start.h.

Moved SECTION_INIT asm void __init_hardware(void) into __ppc_eabi_init.c. This routine enables the floating-point MSR bit, preserves LR in r31, calls __OSPSInit and __OSCacheInit, restores LR, and returns.

Moved SECTION_INIT asm void __flush_cache(void* address, size_t size) into __ppc_eabi_init.c, preserving the cache flush/invalidate instruction sequence with dcbst, sync, icbi, isync, and the rept loop label.
Removed __init_hardware and __flush_cache implementations from __start.c, leaving __start.c focused on startup/data initialization code such as __init_data.
Removed extern declarations for __OSCacheInit and __OSPSInit from __start.h.
Added void __OSCacheInit(void); to OSCache.h and void __OSPSInit(void); to OSInit.h so declarations live with their owning OS subsystems.
Removed the local extern void PPCHalt(void); line from __ppc_eabi_init.c in the shown diff; no replacement usage is visible in the excerpt.
Removed #pragma push / #pragma peephole off / #pragma pop around __init_cpp in __ppc_eabi_init.c in the shown diff.
smart_moves: Used external evidence from other games' debug maps to correct translation-unit ownership rather than only matching local build convenience.
Kept the assembly bodies materially identical while relocating them, reducing behavioral risk and making the PR a structural correction rather than a logic rewrite.
Applied SECTION_INIT to the moved assembly routines in __ppc_eabi_init.c, preserving their intended init-section placement after relocation.
Replaced ad hoc declarations in __start.h with subsystem-local declarations in OSCache.h and OSInit.h, which makes the include graph communicate ownership more clearly.
Moved only the necessary startup helper routines and declarations, keeping the diff small: 5 files changed with balanced additions/deletions.
decomp_lessons: Debug maps from other games can provide useful evidence for assigning functions to the correct translation unit, especially for runtime/startup code where local symbol placement may be uncertain.
When moving a function between TUs, preserve section attributes such as SECTION_INIT; correct section placement can matter as much as C-level behavior for matching.

Internal Dolphin OS routines should be declared in headers associated with their subsystem when possible: cache initialization in OSCache.h and processor-state initialization in OSInit.h rather than in an unrelated startup header.
Relocation fixes may require moving include dependencies and constants with the function. Here, MSR_FP and headers for PPCArch, OSCache, and OSInit followed __init_hardware into __ppc_eabi_init.c.
Keep assembly movement mechanically faithful. For fragile startup/cache code, unchanged instruction order, labels, and register usage are important for both matching and correctness.
A TU-correction PR can improve long-term decomp organization even when the net line count is nearly neutral.
naming_conventions: Startup/runtime EABI initialization code is placed under src/dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.c with double-underscore internal names such as __init_hardware, __flush_cache, __init_user, and __init_cpp.
Dolphin OS internal subsystem initializers use double-underscore OS-prefixed names such as __OSCacheInit and __OSPSInit.
Cache subsystem declarations belong in OSCache.h; OS initialization/processor-state declarations belong in OSInit.h.
The moved low-level routines retain lowercase Metrowerks/EABI-style names __init_hardware and __flush_cache rather than being renamed to project-specific wrappers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Relocated asm routines without changing their instruction sequences, helping preserve codegen/matching characteristics.
Marked the moved asm functions with SECTION_INIT in their new TU to preserve init-section layout.
__init_hardware uses nofralloc, mfmsr/ori/mtmsr with MSR_FP 0x2000, saves LR via mflr r31, calls __OSPSInit and __OSCacheInit, restores LR via mtlr r31, then blr.
__flush_cache aligns/adjusts the address with lis/ori/and/subf/add, loops over cache lines with dcbst, sync, icbi, addic, subic., bge rept, then finishes with isync and blr.
The PR demonstrates that matching can depend on TU assignment informed by map evidence, not just local function body equivalence.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching __ppc_eabi_init.c to see whether this TU placement enabled additional matching or section-order fixes.
Check whether other startup/runtime functions in __start.c were later moved based on debug-map evidence.
Search for uses of __OSCacheInit and __OSPSInit declarations to confirm OSCache.h and OSInit.h became the canonical include locations.
Investigate whether removal of the peephole pragmas around __init_cpp affected matching or reflected no-longer-needed compiler workaround context.
